title: Godetia biloba
taxonomy: Godetia biloba is classified within the family Polemoniaceae, which is a group of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the globe mallow or phlox family. Godetia biloba is native to the Americas and is often found in dry, open areas. It is an annual or perennial plant that produces showy, fragrant flowers in shades of pink, white, and purple. The genus Godetia is part of the larger plant classification system, with Godetia biloba being one of the species within it.
uses: Godetia biloba is primarily known for its ornamental value, but limited information is available on its medicinal uses. However, some studies suggest that the plant may have antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Godetia biloba has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of its medicinal properties. More studies are necessary to fully understand the potential health benefits of using Godetia biloba as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Godetia biloba is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that Godetia biloba may have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which could help protect against certain diseases. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and potential uses. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.

constituents: Godetia biloba contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. parts: Godetia biloba is a plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, for its potential health benefits. preparations: Godetia biloba is used in herbal preparations, although limited information is available on its traditional uses. side_effects: Godetia biloba is a flowering plant native to the Americas, but there is limited information on its side effects in humans. intro: Godetia biloba, also known as the Evening Primrose or Mexican Primrose, is a flowering plant species in the evening primrose family, characterized by its showy, fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Godetia biloba include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Godetia biloba include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
